heading: Game refuge in Ozark National Forest
label: 682 Game refuge in Ozark National Forest
text: The President of the United States is authorized to designate such national forest lands within the Ozark National Forest, within the State of Arkansas, as should, in his discretion, be set aside for the protection of game animals, birds, or fish; and, except under such rules and regulations as the Secretary of Agriculture may from time to time prescribe, it shall be unlawful for any person to hunt, catch, trap, willfully disturb, or kill any kind of game animal, game or nongame bird, or fish, or take the eggs of any such bird on any lands so set aside, or in or on the waters thereof.
notes: Amendments1948—Act , struck out penal provisions (see , Crimes and Criminal Procedure), and inserted provision relating to the unlawfulness in hunting, catching, etc., game animals, etc., under rules and regulations of the Secretary of Agriculture.June 25, 1948section 41 of Title 181945—Act , struck out last sentence which read “No lands within the present limits of the fourth congressional district shall be included in such designations.”Aug. 11, 1945
Effective Date of 1948 AmendmentSection 20 of act , provided that the amendment made by that act is effective .June 25, 1948Sept. 1, 1948
